topic: NP pre-1990 linear drift — c5 boundary-note patch verified; new precision issue on PROS-002 parent-genealogy
change_summary: detail_verdict_length 2184→2647 (+21.2%); sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-28T01:09:01.490Z; obe_skipped=0 (head candidate is non-OBE). The drift is the rewritten Verdict boundary note paragraph that landed in workspace-sync a7188fb 2026-05-18T22:05:54Z (~3.5h after c5 was written). c5 holes_found item proposed option (a) [rewrite boundary note acknowledging PROS-002 inheritance and locating distinction in WIN-row language]; the patch implements option (a) and closes ISS-2124 (decider-self-apply-decider-2026-05-18-21-19). This c6 cycle verifies the patch and looks for new issues introduced.
our_argument_summary: WIN-006 (Std Model Explains): pre-1990 linear NMP drift is non-discriminating (both globe core-mantle convection and the dome aetheric vortex can be fit to the same NOAA NP.xy linear trend); the dome supplies no derivation from dome geometry; the claimed 0.0466 deg/yr rate does not survive reproducibility checks under standard geometric conventions; and the WIN fails the post-hoc test (indexed public dataset, no independent pre-registration, monitor hardcodes target on the same commit as data-fetch). The Verdict boundary note now distinguishes WIN-006 from WIN-007/WIN-022 by language in each WIN row (observation-focused vs thermodynamic-label-laden) rather than by mechanism-absence in the parent — closing the c5 hole.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt:19-22 (PROS-002 parent text, includes Below-threshold/Above-threshold mechanism); raw-text/02-wins.txt:68-69 (WIN-006 row entry — observation-only, no PROS-NNN parent arrow); raw-text/02-wins.txt:71-72 (WIN-007 row, PROS-002 line 22 points → WIN-007 + WIN-036); data/sections.json part2b.html (live-fetch WIN list confirms WIN-006/007 fetch NOAA NP.xy)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome source for WIN-006: 'North Pole Pre-1990 Linear Drift CONFIRMED / Source: NOAA NP.xy dataset. 0.0466 deg/year linear drift from 1590-1990.' That is the entire WIN-006 row in raw-text/02-wins.txt — no per-WIN derivation, no parent-prediction arrow, no formula. The mechanism framing ('aetheric vortex precession below threshold') lives in PROS-002 (raw-text/02-wins.txt:21) — but PROS-002 in the raw text points only to WIN-007 + WIN-036 (line 22: '→ WIN-007 + W010 (WIN-036)'). WIN-006 is not explicitly parented to PROS-002 in the dome WIN list; our review constructs the parent-child relationship for analytical reasons. The 0.0466 deg/yr is asserted as a regression slope over 1590-1990; no derivation path from dome geometry (disc_radius, firmament_height) reaches that number.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Per data/sections.json part2b.html, WIN-006 is one of the four live-fetch WINs (WIN-004, WIN-006, WIN-007, WIN-024). Our detail_verdict_text asserts that 'the monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line' — i.e., the threshold value (0.0466) is hardcoded even though the data fetch IS live. This is consistent with the dome's monitor.py pattern: live data fetch + hardcoded comparison threshold = 'pred==obs' check on bundled fields. The 'live_fetch' tag captures the data-fetch side; the post-hoc paragraph captures the hardcoded-comparison side. monitor.py itself is not present in raw-text/ in this clone, so the 'hardcodes target on same commit as fetch line' assertion cannot be re-verified per-cycle from raw text alone — it carries forward from prior-cycle reviews and matches the dome author's own public OTS-anchored status_history.json scheme.
data_sources_used: NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position dataset (gufm1 pre-1900 reconstruction blend + IGRF series modern era; IGRF-14 current generation)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: PROS-002 frames 'below threshold' / 'above threshold' as a step-function aetheric mechanism, with neither threshold position nor critical exponent specified — any observed slope change can be assigned to either side post-hoc. The pre-1990 'below threshold' regime is unbounded on the slope dimension within PROS-002 (no min/max linear rate stated).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: One minor: c5 review and decider issue ISS-2124 both cited PROS-002 location as 'raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 19-22' — that is the wrong file. PROS-002 is in raw-text/02-wins.txt (lines 19-22 of that file describe PROS-002, lines 14, 24, 29, 34, 39, 43, 47 list other PROS-NNN entries). 03-predictions.txt lines 19-22 are about the August 2026 solar eclipse (E001 Ebro etc.), not PROS-002. The error is internal to our analyst/decider audit trail; it has not propagated into user-visible review text (the current WIN-006 detail_verdict_text correctly cites 'PROS-002' without specifying a filename). Recommend the analyst correct the file reference in future analyses to avoid downstream confusion.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: data/sections.json part2b.html explicitly enumerates the live-fetch WINs and lists 'WIN-006 and WIN-007 (North Magnetic Pole drift, from NOAA NP.xy)' among them. Dome raw-text/02-wins.txt:68-69 cites 'Source: NOAA NP.xy dataset' for WIN-006. The tag carries forward from c5; no contrary evidence this cycle.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard geophysics (core-mantle convection / dynamo modeling; spherical harmonic flux-lobe decomposition; gufm1 + IGRF reconstructions are themselves products of standard dynamo theory) explains the pre-1990 slow NMP drift. The dome's 'aetheric vortex precession below threshold' (PROS-002, raw-text/02-wins.txt:21) renames the same observed trajectory without producing a different predicted slope. The 0.0466 deg/yr value is the dome's regression slope on NOAA-derived data; it is not derived from disc_radius or firmament_height.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: NOAA NGDC NP.xy is an indexed public dataset (gufm1 pre-1900 reconstructions published since the early 2000s; IGRF series since the 1960s, IGRF-14 current generation). No independent pre-registration of 0.0466 deg/yr in any timestamping venue. Per the WIN-006 detail_verdict_text itself, monitor commit ordering does not satisfy the dome author's own ('timestamped before confirming data was pulled') standard.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula path links dome-specific parameters (disc_radius=20,015 km; firmament_height=9,086 km; sun_altitude=5,733 km; moon_altitude=2,534 km) to a predicted NMP drift rate. The 0.0466 deg/yr is asserted to match the NOAA-derived regression slope. Cross-check this cycle: great-circle interpretation 0.0466 × 111 = 5.17 km/yr; longitude-only at 78.9°N = 5.17 × cos(78.9°) ≈ 1.00 km/yr. Either is below the 10-15 km/yr observed pre-1990 range cited in our detail_evidence. Even as a centuries-averaged regression slope over 1590-1990, a 0.0466 deg/yr great-circle rate (5.17 km/yr × 400 yr = 2070 km total) is roughly 3× the actual NMP great-circle displacement over that interval (gufm1 reconstruction NMP at ~74°N 273°E in 1590 vs ~78.9°N 254°E in 1990 = ~729 km great-circle, ≈1.82 km/yr averaged, ≈0.0164 deg/yr). The 0.0466 figure does not reproduce under any standard geometric convention I can construct.

holes_found.description: The patched Verdict boundary note overstates the parent-prediction genealogy. The note now reads: 'WIN-006 uses Std Model Explains rather than Misleading (WIN-007, WIN-022) despite all three sharing the same parent prediction (PROS-002).' But raw-text/02-wins.txt:22 — the entire PROS-002 entry — points only to WIN-007 and WIN-036 ('→ WIN-007 + W010 (WIN-036)'). The dome WIN list (raw-text/02-wins.txt:68-69) lists WIN-006 as a standalone confirmation with no parent-prediction arrow, and PROS-002 does not list WIN-006 among its outputs. WIN-022 is also not pointed to by PROS-002; our review treats WIN-022 as a 'duplicate of WIN-007' on internal grounds. So the 'all three sharing the same parent prediction' claim is something our review constructs for analytical purposes, not something the dome itself asserts. A defender can attack by quoting raw-text/02-wins.txt:22 verbatim and showing PROS-002 has no '→ WIN-006' arrow. This is a precision issue, not a logical break — the underlying argument (mechanism label is shared even if WIN-as-output arrow is not) is intact — but as currently worded it gives the defender an easy 30-second rebuttal.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the opening clause of the boundary note from 'despite all three sharing the same parent prediction (PROS-002: aetheric vortex precession below threshold)' to something like: 'PROS-002 in the dome WIN list (raw-text/02-wins.txt:19-22) names WIN-007 and WIN-036 as its outputs and frames the mechanism (aetheric vortex precession below threshold; runaway above threshold) for the whole pre-1990/post-1990 sequence. WIN-006 reports the pre-1990 linear drift that PROS-002 invokes as its pre-threshold backdrop; the dome lists WIN-006 as a standalone confirmation, but the mechanism label is therefore shared across WIN-006/WIN-007/WIN-022.' Roughly +2 sentences; preserves the verdict-distinction logic while accurately representing the WIN-list genealogy.

holes_found.description: The detail_verdict and tldr_verdict assertion that 0.0466 deg/yr 'cannot be reproduced from NP.xy by any standard metric' remains unspecific. c5 stronger_arguments flagged this; the patch did not address it. A dome defender can argue: 'you tested two metrics (great-circle, longitude-only) and concluded any metric fails — but you never named the metrics, so the defender does not know what survives.' This is the c5 advocate_mode defense_survives=3 finding restated; it is now the strongest remaining attack surface after the boundary-note patch.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'by any standard metric' with 'under any of the three standard geometric conventions (great-circle arc, longitude-only at NMP latitude, latitude-only) — 0.0466 deg/yr converts to 1.0-5.2 km/yr; observed pre-1990 NMP drift is 10-15 km/yr (NOAA historical records), accelerating to 15-18 km/yr in the 1975-1990 decade.' Roughly +2 sentences in detail_verdict_text; for the tldr, even a one-word qualifier like 'by any standard angular-to-linear convention' would tighten the language without lengthening it.

holes_found.description: Duplicated post-hoc paragraph across WIN-006, WIN-007, WIN-022, WIN-036 (all NOAA NP.xy derivations) — same hole flagged in c5 stronger_arguments, not yet acted on. Each WIN replicates ~600 chars of post-hoc boilerplate. TF-IDF dilution; harms signal-to-noise per WIN. Not blocking; verdict logic stands; but worth re-surfacing because the surface area for the duplication has only grown since c5.
holes_found.recommendation: Hoist the duplicated post-hoc paragraph once into Part 3.1b and replace per-WIN copies with a 1-sentence reference (e.g., 'This WIN also fails the post-hoc test for the NOAA NP.xy dataset cluster — see Part 3.1b NOAA NP.xy section'). Risk: hoisting is a structural change touching four WINs; analyst/decider may want to bundle it with the WIN-006/007/022/036 cluster expansion rather than do it piecemeal.

stronger_arguments.description: Re-order detail_verdict to LEAD with quantitative falsification rather than non-discriminating framing. c5 stronger_argument item #1, still unimplemented. Inverting the order — lead with '0.0466 deg/yr does not reproduce under any standard geometric convention,' then follow with 'and even if the number were reproducible, the linear trend is non-discriminating' — makes the argument harder to dismiss. The current order lets a defender concede non-discriminating (which can be reframed as consistent-with-both-models = pro-dome) without confronting the harder numerical claim.
sources: c5 stronger_arguments item #1 (monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-006.c5.json); reproduced this cycle without additional evidence.

stronger_arguments.description: Address the centuries-averaged interpretation directly. A dome defender accepting that 0.0466 deg/yr is below the recent-decade rates can fall back to: '0.0466 deg/yr is the regression slope over 1590-1990, a centuries-averaged number — comparing against 1975-1990 decade rates is unfair.' Our review can pre-empt this by showing 0.0466 deg/yr (great-circle = 5.17 km/yr) implies ~2070 km of NMP displacement over 400 years, whereas gufm1 reconstructions show ~730 km of great-circle NMP displacement 1590-1990 (≈1.82 km/yr, ≈0.0164 deg/yr). The 0.0466 figure is roughly 3× the actual centuries-averaged great-circle drift rate — it does not reproduce under the defender's own preferred interpretation either.
sources: Quantitative cross-check this cycle: gufm1 NMP at ~74°N 273°E (1590) vs ~78.9°N 254°E (1990); great-circle displacement formula returns ~729 km; /400 yr = 1.82 km/yr; ÷111 km/deg = 0.0164 deg/yr — vs dome 0.0466 deg/yr.

kernel_of_truth.description: The pre-1990 NMP drift IS qualitatively slow and roughly linear over century-scale windows — both the dome's PROS-002 framing (raw-text/02-wins.txt:21 'slow linear precession') and the NOAA NGDC gufm1+IGRF reconstructions agree on the qualitative shape. The dome correctly identified a real, well-documented phenomenon and bet on the pre-1990 trajectory remaining roughly linear (data/wins.json:WIN-006.detail_evidence ascribes 10-15 km/yr to pre-1990 drift). Getting the qualitative shape right is itself non-trivial — many flat-Earth claims are wrong even at the descriptive level, and this one is descriptively correct (data/wins.json:WIN-006.tldr_evidence).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Descriptive correctness about slope direction is necessary but not sufficient for confirmation under the dome's own predictive standard (raw-text/01-home.txt 'timestamped before confirming data was pulled,' echoed in data/wins.json:WIN-006.detail_verdict_text). Standard geophysics also describes the slope (data/wins.json:WIN-006.detail_evidence cites core-mantle boundary convection patterns / spherical harmonic models); the slope is non-discriminating. The specific numerical claim — 0.0466 deg/yr — does not survive reproducibility (this cycle: 5.17 km/yr great-circle, 1.00 km/yr longitude rate; even as a centuries-averaged value, the gufm1+IGRF reconstruction gives ~1.82 km/yr / 0.0164 deg/yr — 3× lower than 0.0466). The dome wins the qualitative description and loses the quantitative match (data/wins.json:WIN-006.tldr_verdict).

deeper_analysis:
The c5 patch (decider-self-apply-decider-2026-05-18-21-19 closing ISS-2124) chose option (a) from c5 holes_found: rewrite the boundary note to acknowledge PROS-002 inheritance and locate the verdict distinction in WIN-row language. The patch successfully addresses the c5 concern: the new text no longer claims WIN-006 lacks a mechanism claim in the parent. The new text correctly identifies the distinction as type-of-language inside each WIN row (observation-focused for WIN-006; thermodynamic-label-foregrounding for WIN-007/WIN-022). The 'phase transition' debunk callout (no order parameter / no critical exponent / no symmetry-breaking mechanism) is well-placed and matches WIN-022 detail_verdict_text's existing language.

The new precision issue is that the rewrite went slightly farther than needed and asserted a 'shared parent prediction' relationship across all three WINs (WIN-006/WIN-007/WIN-022). The dome's WIN list does not support that relationship literally — PROS-002 has explicit '→ WIN-007 + WIN-036' arrows in raw-text/02-wins.txt:22, with no arrow to WIN-006 or WIN-022. The mechanism FRAMING is shared (PROS-002's text describes both pre- and post-1990 behavior), but the WIN-as-output GENEALOGY is not. A defender can reasonably quote the raw text and argue: 'WIN-006 is a standalone observation; you are constructing the parent-child relationship for analytical convenience.' That is true but not fatal — our review can own the construction explicitly, which is the holes_found item #1 recommendation.

The c5 advocate_mode finding (defense_survives=3, 'any standard metric' is sweeping and a defender can call it under-specified) has not been addressed and is now the strongest remaining attack surface after the boundary-note patch. Holes_found item #2 captures this.

The c5 cross-WIN-consistency finding (compatible:false, boundary rationale needs sharpening) is now partially closed — the boundary rationale IS sharper — but with the new precision issue layered on top. Net: the c5 'compatible:false' becomes 'compatible:true with precision caveat' in c6.

Verdict: Std Model Explains continues to hold. Recommended action: minor_edit (boundary-note opening clause precision; 'any standard metric' qualifier; both fixes are ~2 sentences total).

advocate_mode.best_defense: A defender accepting our six methodological principles (especially discriminatory-power-required and no-strawmanning) reads the new boundary note and the rest of WIN-006 and constructs: '(1) Your boundary note says WIN-006 and WIN-007/022 share PROS-002 as a parent prediction. Quote the actual PROS-002 entry from raw-text/02-wins.txt:22 — it points to WIN-007 and WIN-036, not WIN-006 and not WIN-022. WIN-006 is a standalone observation in the dome WIN list. Your boundary distinction is internally consistent if you judge each WIN by its own row text — but you should own that you are choosing the WIN row as the unit of judgment, not asserting a parent-child relationship the dome WIN list does not show. (2) On the numerical falsification, your tldr_verdict says 0.0466 deg/yr cannot be reproduced by any standard metric. You name only two metrics in your detail (great-circle, longitude-only). You have not addressed the centuries-averaged-regression interpretation: 0.0466 deg/yr × 400 yr = 18.64 deg total, which is a plausible magnitude for the 1590-1990 NMP great-circle displacement at face value. Your 10-15 km/yr figure in detail_evidence is a recent-decades rate, not the regression slope. The numerical mismatch may dissolve if interpreted on the timescale the dome explicitly names (1590-1990).' (Note: a tighter cross-check using gufm1 NMP positions actually refutes the centuries-averaged defense too — 729 km over 400 years is ≈0.0164 deg/yr great-circle, well below 0.0466 — but our review text does not contain that cross-check, so the defender can press the centuries-averaged interpretation until we add the numbers.)
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Both fixes in holes_found items #1 and #2. Item #1 closes (1) — own the parent-child construction explicitly and reference raw-text/02-wins.txt:22 verbatim. Item #2 closes (2) — name the three geometric conventions and give the centuries-averaged conversion (0.0466 deg/yr ≈ 5.17 km/yr great-circle vs gufm1 reconstruction's ~1.82 km/yr centuries-averaged). Combined ~4-5 sentences across detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict; not a structural change.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: c5 had compatible:false on the grounds that the boundary rationale couldn't survive a quote of PROS-002. The patched boundary note now addresses this — it explicitly acknowledges the shared mechanism framing — so compatibility flips to true. The remaining precision issue (over-asserting parent-child genealogy) is captured in holes_found item #1; it is a wording problem, not a cross-WIN logical inconsistency. WIN-007/WIN-022/WIN-036 detail_verdict_text references all checked this cycle; the boundary note's characterizations match the target WINs' own text (WIN-007 'aetheric threshold crossing', WIN-022 'phase transition is a category error: no order parameter, no symmetry breaking, no critical exponents'). The cluster is now internally consistent.

claims_checked: Dome 0.0466 deg/yr vs observed 10-15 km/yr pre-1990 (broad) and 15-18 km/yr 1975-1990 decade
Centuries-averaged-regression interpretation: implied total displacement under 0.0466 deg/yr × 400 yr vs gufm1+IGRF reconstruction NMP displacement 1590-1990
quantitative_verification.computations: (1) Direct conversions: 0.0466 deg/yr × 111 km/deg = 5.17 km/yr great-circle. At NMP-1990 latitude ~78.9°N, longitude-only rate = 5.17 × cos(78.9°) = 1.00 km/yr. Latitude-only rate at the same latitude = 5.17 km/yr (assuming pure latitude-direction drift, which is not the observed direction). Our detail_evidence pre-1990 range is 10-15 km/yr → mismatch by factor of 2-15× depending on convention. (2) Centuries-averaged: gufm1 reconstruction puts NMP near 74°N 273°E in 1590, IGRF puts it near 78.9°N 254°E in 1990. Spherical law of cosines great-circle distance = R × arccos(sin lat1 sin lat2 + cos lat1 cos lat2 cos Δlon) = 6371 × arccos(sin(74°)sin(78.9°) + cos(74°)cos(78.9°)cos(19°)) ≈ 729 km. Averaged over 400 yr = 1.82 km/yr ≈ 0.0164 deg/yr great-circle. Dome's 0.0466 deg/yr is ~3× this. So 0.0466 fails even under the defender's preferred centuries-averaged interpretation.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The dome value (0.0466 deg/yr) does not reproduce under any of three standard geometric conventions (great-circle 5.17, longitude-only 1.00, latitude-only 5.17 km/yr) when compared against our detail_evidence pre-1990 rates (10-15 km/yr). The dome value is also ~3× the centuries-averaged 1590-1990 great-circle rate. Our review text could foreground these specific conversions to preempt the 'unspecified metric' attack — see holes_found item #2. Caveat: my 1590-1990 endpoint positions (74°N 273°E and 78.9°N 254°E) are approximate gufm1+IGRF figures, not exact dataset reads; the resulting ≈729 km is a rough order-of-magnitude check, not a precise displacement.

summary_for_decider: WIN-006 c6: verdict Std Model Explains continues to hold. The c5 patch (ISS-2124 closure, decider-self-apply-2026-05-18-21-19) addresses the c5 hole on boundary-note fragility — the new text correctly identifies the verdict distinction as type-of-language within each WIN row, not absence-of-mechanism in the parent. Two new minor/moderate issues: (1) MODERATE — the boundary note overstates parent-prediction genealogy (claims WIN-006/007/022 share PROS-002 as parent, but raw-text/02-wins.txt:22 shows PROS-002 → WIN-007 + WIN-036 only; WIN-006 is a standalone WIN-list entry with no parent arrow); recommend 2-sentence rewrite owning the analytical construction. (2) MINOR — 'any standard metric' tldr framing remains unspecific; recommend naming the three geometric conventions and giving the centuries-averaged conversion (0.0466 deg/yr ≈ 5.17 km/yr great-circle vs gufm1 reconstruction's ~1.82 km/yr centuries-averaged — defender's preferred interpretation still fails). Two stronger_arguments carried forward from c5 (lead-with-numerical-falsification; hoist duplicated post-hoc paragraph). One internal-audit citation error flagged (c5 review and ISS-2124 cited PROS-002 as living in 03-predictions.txt rather than 02-wins.txt; not exposed in user-visible review). No carry-over open issues; no verdict change.
